task_completion.description: Log in to the ArkCase application
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the ArkCase dashboard or home screen, indicating a successful login (e.g., user profile icon or welcome message visible).
The 'Complaints' module is selected in the application's navigation menu or sidebar, and the Complaints list or dashboard is visible.

task_completion.description: Configure a search filter for 'High' priority cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: A search, filter, or advanced search panel is open with the 'Priority' field explicitly set to 'High'.

task_completion.description: Save the search with the name 'Urgent Safety Queue'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Search' dialog box showing the name 'Urgent Safety Queue' being entered, or a saved searches dropdown/list displaying 'Urgent Safety Queue'.

task_completion.description: Execute the search and view the matching cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search results page is visible, displaying exactly 3 cases that match the 'High' priority filter.
A text editor or terminal window showing the file '/home/ga/urgent_cases.txt' containing exactly 3 case numbers, one per line.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with ArkCase via a web browser GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Chromium or Firefox) open to the ArkCase web interface, rather than terminal curl/API commands being used to manipulate the system.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the search to find the case numbers
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be at least one screenshot showing the ArkCase GUI displaying the search results with the specific case numbers before they appear in the text file.

integrity.description: The output text file was created manually or via standard terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) or standard terminal commands (like echo or cat) should be visible creating the '/home/ga/urgent_cases.txt' file, rather than a script automatically generating it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: ArkCase is a web-based case management system. The agent must log in and navigate to the 'Complaints' module. The system contains exactly 3 'High' priority cases and 2 'Low' priority cases. The agent must create a saved search named exactly 'Urgent Safety Queue' that filters for 'High' priority. The final output must be a text file at '/home/ga/urgent_cases.txt' containing exactly 3 case numbers (one per line) corresponding to the 'High' priority cases.
